description: The condition and course of being alive are 'lioru'. The word stays with life as lived rather than positing an unseen essence. 'lima' (alive) states a condition, while 'liona' (live) presents the activity or way of dwelling.
articulatory_notes: The tongue touches the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and opens into high-front 'i'. Stressed rounded 'o' stands alone across the first hiatus; a tap or brief trill then leads into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'li' is shared deliberately with 'lima' (alive) and 'liona' (live). All three begin on the same lateral and high vowel, which keeps the family audible.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Attention to a finite life can deepen care without requiring agreement about a soul or life force. The noun stays with the life that can be encountered and affected.

semantic_domains.nature: the condition shared by living beings
semantic_domains.temporal: a life considered as a course through time
